title: Cube Drawer Bench
summary: A square entryway bench box with a single wide drawer on slides — a framed 1x18 cube, faced with 1x2 legs and trim, that hides real storage in one drawer rather than a lid.
description:
This bench is a plain framed box — 1x18 boards (wide project panels or plywood ripped to width) forming the top, bottom, and sides — with a single wide drawer riding on slides mounted to 1x2 spacers set to fit inside the face frame. Building the box perfectly square is called out directly as the step that decides whether the drawer glides or sticks: check square before moving on to anything else.

The legs attach to the front and back of the bench with their tops kept flush, fastened at the drawer slide supports as well as the box itself for real rigidity — if you have a pocket-hole jig, building the leg frame as its own assembly first and then attaching the whole thing with finish nails is the cleaner sequence. Trim boards cap the top and bottom, and are worth cutting to your actual measurement rather than the plan number for a tight, gap-free fit.

Build the drawer as a simple framed box — sides to bottom, then back — and dry-fit it fully into the bench on its slides before attaching the face. **Center the drawer face on the opening with the drawer fully inserted**, not before, since a face attached to a partially-open drawer will land off-center once the drawer seats home.

tools.note: If ripping plywood to 18" width instead of buying project panels.

materials.name: 1x18 project panel or plywood, 10 ft equivalent
Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.material: 1x18 project panel or plywood, 10 ft equivalent
1x18 project panel or plywood, 10 ft equivalent

cutList.note: Cut to your actual assembled measurement.

cutList.material: 1x18 project panel or plywood, 10 ft equivalent

steps.title: Build the bench box
steps.body: Build the box from the top, bottom, and sides using pocket hole screws and glue, or finish nails/countersunk screws and glue if you don't have a jig. Check for square before continuing — this bench needs to be very square or the drawer will stick.
materials: 1x18 project panel or plywood, 10 ft equivalent

steps.title: Add the drawer slide supports
steps.body: Consult your drawer slides for placement, then attach the 1x2 slide supports as spacers to position the slide inside the face frame.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to the front and back of the bench, keeping tops flush, and fasten to the drawer slide supports as well. Use 2" finish nails or screws and glue — or build the leg frame separately first if using a pocket-hole jig, then attach with finish nails.

steps.title: Trim the bench and add the back
steps.body: Trim out the top and bottom of the bench, cutting each trim piece to your actual assembled measurement for a tight fit. Attach the back panel.

steps.title: Build the drawer
steps.body: Attach the drawer sides to the bottom, then attach the drawer back — screwing into both the bottom and sides. Use 2" screws and glue, or 1-1/4" pocket hole screws.
materials: 1x18 project panel or plywood, 10 ft equivalent

steps.title: Install the drawer and attach the face
steps.body: Attach the drawer to the bench with the drawer slides, fully inserting it into the box. With the drawer fully pushed in, center the face on the opening and secure with 2" finish nails. Add drawer pulls, sand, and finish.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: Square entryway bench with a single wide drawer
